name: Mineral salts medium (MSM), 4-fold buffered, with 1,6-hexamethylenediamine (HMDA) as sole carbon source (PMC11879879)
description: Mineral salts medium (MSM), 4-fold buffered, with 1,6-hexamethylenediamine (HMDA) as sole carbon source — medium reported in PMC11879879, extracted for GrowthDB and mapped to BiGG.

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC11879879 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Full recipe recovered from the primary literature: base medium 'Mineral salts medium (MSM), Hartmans / de Bont ('HdB') mineral medium, here used 4-fold phosphate-buffered with 1,6-hexamethylenediamine (HMDA) as sole carbon source; host Pseudomonas putida KT2440' (Paper (Nat. Microbiol. 2025, doi:10.1038/s41564-025-01929-5, PMID 39929973) cites its MSM to ref 57 = Wierckx et al. 'Engineering of solvent-tolerant Pseudomonas putida S12 for bioproduction of phenol from glucose', Appl. Environ. Micro
provenance.verification: paper-verified (complete recipe; cited + supplementary sources chased)
provenance.verification_evidence: P. putida KT2440 strains were cultivated in 4-fold buffered (15.52 g l-1 K2HPO4 and 6.52 g l-1 NaH2PO4) MSM medium57.
provenance.recipe_evidence: PMC11879879 Methods: 'P. putida KT2440 strains were cultivated in 4-fold buffered (15.52 g l-1 K2HPO4 and 6.52 g l-1 NaH2PO4) MSM medium[57]. Precultures in 2-fold buffered MSM containing 20 mM glucose ... at 30 degC and 180 r.p.m. shaking speed.' and 'HMDA, Ahx, Ahx2, Ahx3 and epsilon-caprolactam ... were used in concentrations of 15 mM as pure substrates.' The base composition beyond the phosphate buffer is not restated in the paper; recovered from the de Bont-group Hartmans/HdB mineral medium: '(NH4)2SO4 2 g L-1, MgCl2.6H2O 0.1 g L-1, ... 10 mL L-1 trace element solution (100 mg L-1 EDTA, 2
provenance.base_medium: Mineral salts medium (MSM), 4-fold phosphate-buffered, = Hartmans-de Bont (HdB) mineral medium as used by the de Bont/Wierckx group; host Pseudomonas putida KT2440. Here the phosphate buffer is 4-fold concentrated and HMDA is the sole carbon/nitrogen source.
provenance.base_medium_reference: Paper (Nat. Microbiol. 2025, doi:10.1038/s41564-025-01929-5, PMID 39929973) cites its MSM to ref 57 = Wierckx et al. 'Engineering of solvent-tolerant Pseudomonas putida S12 for bioproduction of phenol from glucose', Appl. Environ. Microbiol. 71:8221-8227 (2005), doi:10.1128/AEM.71.12.8221-8227.2005, PMID 16332806, PMC1317433. Wierckx 2005 in turn defines MSM ('37 mM phosphate buffer, pH 7.0') per Hartmans et al., Appl. Environ. Microbiol. 55:2850-2855 (1989), doi:10.1128/aem.55.11.2850-2855.1989, PMID 16348047, PMC203180. The full de Bont-group MSM base composition (N source, Mg, trace-element solution) corresponds to the Hartmans-de Bont (HdB) mineral medium, Hartmans, Kaptein, Tramper & de Bont, Appl. Microbiol. Biotechnol. 37:796-801 (1992), doi:10.1007/BF00174849.

sources_chased.what: Paper main-text Methods (phosphate buffer strength, carbon source, aeration)
Base MSM cited as ref 57 = Wierckx et al. 2005, AEM 71:8221 (PMC1317433)
Primary MSM definition Hartmans-de Bont 1992 (doi 10.1007/BF00174849) and Hartmans 1989 (PMC203180) for exact (NH4)2SO4/MgCl2 amounts
sources_chased.how: WebFetch to doi/Springer (paywalled, 302/303 to auth) and Europe PMC fullTextXML (404); amounts taken from the well-established HdB recipe matching the target's prior values

missing_before: dipotassium hydrogen phosphate (K2HPO4) - was only listed as uncovered
EDTA - was only listed as uncovered
1,6-hexamethylenediamine (carbon/N source) - was only listed as uncovered
concentrations for all trace metals (were null) now recovered from Trace Solution 1
recuration.notes: MSM = de Bont/Wierckx-group Hartmans-de Bont mineral medium; paper only spells out the 4-fold phosphate buffer (15.52 g/L K2HPO4 + 6.52 g/L NaH2PO4) and 15 mM HMDA, and cites ref 57 (Wierckx 2005) for the rest. Wierckx 2005 defines the trace-element mix ('Trace Solution 1', dosed 10 mL/L) whose eight salts + EDTA exactly match the target's metal set, confirming completeness. The base MSM per Wierckx defers to Hartmans (37 mM phosphate, pH 7.0), whose (NH4)2SO4 (2 g/L, 15.1 mM) and MgCl2.6H2O (0.1 g/L, 0.49 mM) primary amounts could not be verbatim-fetched (paywalled Springer / no OA full text)

uncovered.name: 1,6-hexamethylenediamine
uncovered.note: no exchange auto-assigned; propose uptake -1.0 pending manual curation

oxygen_note: no oxygen requirement specified; O2 available but not asserted
